title: HerraduraKEx Protocol Specification
description: Machine-readable inventory of protocols, PEM wire-format labels, CLI --algo tags, security-level classification, and parameter sizes for the HerraduraKEx cryptographic suite. Generated by spec/generate_spec.py from the canonical source files listed in `generated_from` -- see spec/README.md. Consumers (tools or LLMs) should treat this file, not prose documentation, as the source of truth for exact wire-format strings and algo tags.

spec_version.description: Version of this spec document's structure (independent of the suite's own version).

generated_from.description: Source files this spec was mechanically generated from or cross-checked against.

wire_format_labels.description: PEM block labels (HerraduraCli/herradura_codec.h PEM_* constants) not already covered as a protocol's private/public key label -- session keys, signatures, ciphertexts, proofs, protocol-specific artifacts.

cli_subcommands.description: Per-subcommand --algo value lists, keyed by subcommand name, as accepted by at least one of the three CLI implementations.

cross_implementation_gaps.description: Known feature-parity gaps between the C, Go, and Python CLIs (an algo tag or subcommand present in one but not another). Not exhaustive of every edge case -- see spec/README.md.

parameters.description: Protocol-family parameter sizes extracted from herradura.h / herradura/herradura.go constants (key bit widths, Stern-F/ZKP-NL/WOTS/HKEX-RNL round and dimension counts).

id.description: The --algo tag used across CLI subcommands and (for key-producing algorithms) as the key in genpkey's algo table.

pem_labels.description: PEM block labels this protocol's keys/artifacts use, when produced via genpkey/pkey.

cli_support.description: Which of the three CLI implementations accept this algo tag for at least one subcommand.

parameters.description: Protocol-specific parameter sizes (bit widths, round counts, etc). Keys vary by protocol family; see spec/README.md for the constant each key maps to.

status.description: production: suitable for its stated (classical or PQC) security claim at shipped parameters. demo-only: shipped parameters are intentionally below production soundness/security (see notes for the production parameter). pedagogical: exists to illustrate a construction, not to be used at all in a real deployment. deprecated: superseded, no further work planned. broken: a proof or exploit shows the construction fails its security goal. research: proposed/prototyped, not a shipped CLI algo.

quantum_resistant.description: true/false, or a string like "conjectured" when the claim is not proven.

algos.description: Maps each accepted --algo value to the list of CLI implementations that accept it for this subcommand.
